judgments.reasoning: No candidate covers try-catch around dynamic import for error handling; C6 is about lazy loading, not import failure handling.
C1 identifies the same forEach(async) fire-and-forget antipattern with dropped promises and missing error propagation.
No candidate addresses the naming inconsistency between the TwoFactor export and the BackupCode.tsx filename.
No candidate flags the incorrect error message text referencing 'backup code login' on the disable endpoint.
C1 identifies the same case-sensitive backup code comparison via indexOf without toLowerCase in the login authorize path.
C3 identifies the same TOCTOU non-atomic read-then-update race allowing concurrent requests to both pass with the same backup code.
C1 identifies the same unguarded .integration access on potentially-undefined mainHostDestinationCalendar after empty-array destructure.
No candidate addresses the redundant optional chaining on mainHostDestinationCalendar?.integration when the ternary already guards it.
C2 identifies the same tautological .find() where the else-branch compares externalId against the already-falsy externalCalendarId.
No candidate covers the IS_TEAM_BILLING_ENABLED logic inversion causing both slug and requestedSlug to be set when billing is enabled.
No candidate addresses the Calendar interface createEvent signature contract break for implementations like Lark/Office365.
No candidates provided for this PR.
No candidates provided for this PR.
C2 identifies the same forEach with async callbacks (deleteScheduledEmailReminder/SMS) as fire-and-forget with silently dropped promise rejections.
C1 identifies the same issue: immediateDelete=true path cancels the send but does not delete or mark the DB row, leaving orphaned WorkflowReminder rows.
C1 identifies the same bug: end boundary of working hours uses slotStartTime instead of slotEndTime.
C2 identifies the same === reference comparison on Dayjs objects for override check that always returns false.
C1 identifies the same issue: parseRefreshTokenResponse assigns the literal string 'refresh_token' as the token value.
No candidate addresses the invalid Zod schema syntax using computed property keys.
C2 identifies the same issue: parseRefreshTokenResponse returns a SafeParseReturnType wrapper stored into DB instead of the resolved credential keys.
C4 identifies the same issue: in sync mode the raw fetch Response is returned and the HubspotToken cast silently mismatches, making .data undefined.
C3 identifies the same issue: sync-mode returns raw fetch Response so .data.error and .data access crash in zoho-bigin and zohocrm callers.
No candidate addresses the stale-read concurrency issue with retryCount increment; C2 describes a distinct re-increment scenario.
C1 identifies the same deleteMany OR branch that incorrectly deletes non-SMS workflow reminders with retryCount > 1.
C2 identifies the same case-sensitive blacklist email comparison allowing uppercase input to bypass the check.
C1 identifies the same isTeamAdminOrOwner using && instead of || blocking all non-owner team admins.
C3 identifies the same issue: sendAddGuestsEmails called with original guests list instead of uniqueGuests.
No candidate addresses the missing deduplication within the input list in uniqueGuests filter.
No candidate addresses the initial array containing an empty string causing validation issues.
No candidate addresses the empty data object in updateManyByCredentialId preventing Prisma @updatedAt from triggering.
C1 identifies the same macOS-only sed -i '' syntax that fails on GNU/Linux systems.
C1 identifies the same duplicate self.downsize definition where the 3-arg form silently replaces the 5-arg form.
C2 identifies the same hardcoded 10MB client-side preflight that bypasses SiteSettings dynamic max size.
No candidate addresses the animated GIF path failing when 80% dimensions is passed.
C1 identifies the same nil dereference crash when no TopicUser record exists for the current user.
No candidate identifies the stopNotificiationsText typo (missing 'n' in Notifications).
No candidate addresses the should_block_email? side effect of updating statistics during what should be a read-only check.
C2 identifies the same regex lacking a trailing anchor, enabling whitelist bypass on domain-suffix variations.
C6 identifies the same SSRF vulnerability via open-uri with an unvalidated URL in poll_feed and import_remote.
C1 identifies the same insufficient indexOf substring origin check in embed.js that can be spoofed with a malicious domain.
No candidate addresses postMessage targetOrigin using the full referrer URL instead of just scheme+host+port.
No candidate flags X-Frame-Options: ALLOWALL completely disabling clickjacking protection.
No candidate addresses the potential NoMethodError when contents is nil in TopicEmbed.import.
C2 identifies the same invalid 'end if' ERB syntax in best.html.erb that raises SyntaxError on every render.
No candidate addresses the float:left and flexbox mixing causing layout issues.
C1 identifies the same fabricated -ms-align-items property that never existed in any IE/Edge version.
No candidate identifies the missing ? suffix on include_website_name required by Rails serializer conditional include protocol.
No candidate addresses the specific .topic-meta-data h5 a color change replacing scale-color with dark-light-choose.
C4 identifies the same lightness change from 30% to 50% in desktop/user.scss card-content name color for the light theme.
C1 covers topic-post.scss but reports opposite direction (30% to 70%) vs G3's stated 70% to 30%; not a confident match.
C3 identifies the same setupController calling model.findMembers() fire-and-forget without await, leaving members empty on first render.
C4 identifies the same next action clamping offset to user_count rather than last valid page start, producing an empty page.
